questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: A small amount of a powder can dissolve in water to form a clear solution. When this solution is mixed with  \( K_{2}CO_{3}(aq) \) , a white precipitate is obtained. What can the powder be?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In an oxide of metal M, the mass percentage of M is 55.0%. What is the chemical formula of this oxide?

(Relative atomic masses: O = 16.0, M = 39.1)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning  \( CH_{3}COOH \)  and HCl is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: \( CH_{3}COOH \)  is a stronger acid than HCl.

options.label: B
options.text: The pH of 0.1 M  \( CH_{3}COOH(aq) \)  is lower than that of 0.1 M HCl(aq).

options.label: C
options.text: Both  \( CH_{3}COOH(aq) \)  and HCl(aq) react with  \( NH_{3}(aq) \) , each giving a salt.

options.label: D
options.text: Both  \( CH_{3}COOH(aq) \)  and HCl(aq) react with Ag(s), each giving a colourless gas.

Substance Standard enthalpy change of combustion at 298 K / kJ mol \( ^{-1} \)

C(graphite) -394
CH \( _{3} \) CH \( _{2} \) OH(l) -1371

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Silicon and carbon react to form silicon carbide. The crystal structure of silicon carbide is similar to that of diamond. Silicon carbide is very hard because

options.label: A
options.text: it has a high melting point.

options.label: B
options.text: silicon atoms and carbon atoms form triple bonds.

options.label: C
options.text: it has a giant network structure with strong covalent bonds.

options.label: D
options.text: both silicon and carbon atoms have four outermost shell electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning an aluminium ore consisting mainly of  \( Al_{2}O_{3} \)  is correct?
(Relative atomic masses: O = 16.0, Al = 27.0)

options.label: A
options.text: Carbon can be used to extract aluminium from this ore.

options.label: B
options.text: The abundance of this ore in the earth crust is very low.

options.label: C
options.text: This ore contains more than 55% of aluminium by mass.

options.label: D
options.text: Aluminium can be extracted from this ore due to the advancement of technology in applying electricity.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
A sample of 1.02 g of potassium hydrogenphthalate (C₈H₅O₄K) is dissolved completely in distilled water, and then diluted to 250.0 cm³. What is the concentration of the solution obtained?

(Relative atomic masses: H = 1.0, C = 12.0, O = 16.0, K = 39.1)

The systematic name of X is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following molecules is planar?

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
The tendency of being reduced of six ionic species increases in the order as shown below :

\(Ba^{2+}(aq)\) < \(Na^{+}(aq)\) < \(Mg^{2+}(aq)\) < \(H^{+}(aq)\) < \(Cu^{2+}(aq)\) < \(Hg^{2+}(aq)\)

Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: B
options.text: Na(s) has a stronger reducing power than Hg(l).

options.label: C
options.text: \(Hg^{2+}(aq)\) is the weakest oxidising agent among the six species.

options.label: D
options.text: Displacement reaction occurs when  \( \mathrm{Cu(s)} \)  is immersed in  \( \mathrm{MgSO_{4}(aq)} \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following pairs of reactants would react in water to give out the largest amount of heat?

options.label: A
options.text: 1 mol of HCl and 1 mol of KOH

options.label: B
options.text: 1 mol of  \( H_{2}SO_{4} \)  and 2 mol of KOH

options.label: C
options.text: 1 mol of  \( (\mathrm{COOH})_{2} \)  and 2 mol of KOH

options.label: D
options.text: 1 mol of  \( CH_{3}COOH \)  and 1 mol of KOH

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning an  \( {}^{131} \) I atom and a  \( {}^{131} \) Xe atom is / are correct?

(1) They have the same number of protons.
(2) They have different numbers of neutrons.
(3) They have different numbers of outermost shell electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following combinations is / are correct?

Object | Corresponding corrosion prevention method / principle
(1) aluminium window frames | cathodic protection
(2) galvanised iron buckets | sacrificial protection
(3) tin-plated iron cans | alloying

questions.question_text:
The diagram below shows the set-up of an experiment :
[IMG:0.jpg]
The unglazed porcelain in tube A is strongly heated and the glass wool is occasionally heated. Which of the following statements is / are correct?
(1) A chemical reaction occurs at the glass wool.
(2) There is NO colour change in the solution in tube B.
(3) There is NO colour change in the solution in tube C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Which of the following statements concerning a hydrogen-oxygen fuel cell is / are correct?

(1) It produces non-polluting product.
(2) The membrane in it selectively allows hydroxide ions to pass through.
(3) It can continuously produce electricity as long as hydrogen and oxygen are supplied under operating conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
In which of the following processes would a colourless gas evolve?

(1) Magnesium is added to dilute sulphuric acid.
(2) Ammonium chloride is heated with calcium hydroxide.
(3) Water is added to a solid mixture of citric acid and sodium hydrogencarbonate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Which of the following methods can be used to distinguish between  \( ZnCl_{2}(aq) \)  and  \( CaBr_{2}(aq) \) ?

(1) adding  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)
(2) performing flame test
(3) evaporating to dryness

questions.question_text:

(1) mixing methanol and ethanol
(2) mixing chlorine and methane under sunlight
(3) mixing ethene and acidified \(KMnO_{4}(aq)\)

questions.question_text: When iron and copper are separately immersed in hexane completely, iron corrodes faster than copper.

questions.question_text:

Iron can be oxidised more readily than copper.
Burning fossil fuels produces carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of an organic compound is shown below :
[IMG:4.jpg]

Which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The compound does NOT show enantiomerism.

options.label: B
options.text: The molecular formula of the compound is  \( C_{5}H_{6}O_{4} \)

options.label: C
options.text: The compound contains a ketone group.

options.label: D
options.text: The compound can be oxidised by acidified  \( K_{2}Cr_{2}O_{7}(aq) \) .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements concerning compound U  \( \left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\mathrm{CH}_{2}\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CHCH}_{2}\mathrm{CH}_{2}\mathrm{OH}\right) \)  is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The empirical formula of U is  \( C_{3}H_{6}O \) .

options.label: B
options.text: The systematic name of U is hex-4-en-1-ol.

options.label: C
options.text: U reacts with HCl to give a single product.

options.label: D
options.text: U can separately turn  \( Br_{2}(aq) \)  and acidified  \( KMnO_{4}(aq) \)  colourless.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
The structure of a compound is shown below :
[IMG:0.jpg]
(1) It can form a salt with aqueous ammonia.
(2) It can be reduced to an alkanol by using  \( LiAlH_{4} \) .
(3) It can form an ester with methanol under suitable conditions.

Which of the following statements concerning the compound are correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Both statements are true and the 2nd statement is a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: B
options.text: Both statements are true but the 2nd statement is NOT a correct explanation of the 1st statement.

options.label: C
options.text: The 1st statement is false but the 2nd statement is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Increasing reaction temperature can increase the yield for all reversible chemical reactions.
Increasing reaction temperature can shorten the time needed to attain equilibrium for all reversible chemical reactions.

2-Chlorobut-1-ene has a double bond.
